steps.title: Identify the known angles in the triangle
steps.content: From the diagram, the right angle is at $B$ (marked with a small square), so $\angle B = 90°$. The angle at $A$ is labeled as $56°$. We need to find $\angle C$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in any triangle equals $180°$:
$$\angle A + \angle B + \angle C = 180°$$
$$56° + 90° + \angle C = 180°$$
$$\angle C = 180° - 146° = 34°$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
